data.title: Franklin Universal Trust (“FT” or the “Fund”) Announces Distribution
data.description:
Franklin Universal Trust (“FT” or the “Fund”) Announces Distribution

Franklin Universal Trust [NYSE: FT] today announced a monthly distribution from net investment income of $0.0425 per share, payable on August 29, 2025, to shareholders of record on August 18, 2025 (Ex-Dividend Date: August 18, 2025).

The Fund’s primary investment objective is to provide high, current income consistent with preservation of capital. Its secondary objective is growth of income through dividend increases and capital appreciation. Distributions may vary based on the Fund’s net investment income. Past distributions are not indicative of future trends.

Shareholders should not draw any conclusions about the Fund’s investment performance from the amount of the current distribution or from the terms of the Fund’s distribution policy. The amounts and sources of distributions reported herein are only estimates and are not being provided for tax reporting purposes. The actual amounts and sources of the amounts for tax reporting purposes will depend upon the Fund’s investment experience during the remainder of its fiscal year and may be subject to changes based on tax regulations. The Fund will send a Form 1099-DIV to shareholders for the calendar year that will describe how to report the Fund’s distributions for federal income tax purposes.

For further information on Franklin Universal Trust, please visit our web site at: www.franklintempleton.com

Franklin Resources, Inc. is a global investment management organization with subsidiaries operating as Franklin Templeton and serving clients in over 150 countries. Franklin Templeton’s mission is to help clients achieve better outcomes through investment management expertise, wealth management and technology solutions. Through its specialist investment managers, the company offers specialization on a global scale, bringing extensive capabilities in fixed income, equity, alternatives and multi-asset solutions. With more than 1,500 investment professionals, and offices in major financial markets around the world, the California-based company has over 75 years of investment experience and $1.61 trillion in assets under management as of June 30, 2025. For more information, please visit franklintempleton.com.

Franklin Templeton
1-800-342-5236

data.title: Nordic Shares Gain; Tomra Systems Tops Leaders
data.description:
This article was automatically generated by Dow Jones using technology from Automated Insights.

Nordic stocks gained Thursday, with the OMX Nordic 40 Index rising 2.3%.

Tomra Systems ASA was the biggest leader among large stocks during the session, increasing 8.5%, and Lundin Mining Corp. increased 8.3%. Novo Nordisk A/S rounded out the top three leaders on Thursday, with shares gaining 6.7%.

Paradox Interactive AB posted the largest decline, dropping 4.3%, followed by Hafnia Ltd. shares, which fell 3.9%. Shares of Saab AB Series B dropped 3.5%.

In Denmark, the OMX Copenhagen 20 Index rose 3.9%, and the OMX Helsinki 25 Index, which tracks Finland's most-traded stocks, gained 1.0%.

Stocks on Sweden's OMX Stockholm 30 Index increased 1.1%, while Norway's Oslo Exchange Benchmark Index_GI fell 0.2%. Shares in Iceland increased, with the OMX Iceland All-Share PI Equity Index gaining 0.1%.

Elsewhere in Europe, indexes were mixed, with the STOXX Europe 600 Index adding 0.9% and the FTSE 100 Index dropping 0.7% from the previous close.

In commodities news, Brent crude oil futures were down 0.5%, while gold futures were up 0.6%. Bitcoin rose 1.2% to $116,708.

On the currency front, the WSJ Dollar Index was flat at 95.43.

Against the euro, the Swedish krona was up 0.3%, the U.S. dollar was up 0.2%, the Danish krone was flat, the Icelandic krona was flat, and the Norwegian krone was down 0.4%.

Against the U.S. dollar, the Swedish krona was up 0.2%, the Danish krone was down 0.2%, the euro was down 0.2%, the Icelandic krona was down 0.2%, and the Norwegian krone was down 0.6%.

Data source: Dow Jones Market Data, FactSet 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  August 07, 2025 12:21 ET (16:21 GMT)


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: Templeton Emerging Markets Income Fund (“TEI” or the “Fund”) Announces Distribution
data.description:
Templeton Emerging Markets Income Fund (“TEI” or the “Fund”) Announces Distribution

Templeton Emerging Markets Income Fund [NYSE: TEI] today announced a monthly distribution from net investment income of $0.0475 per share, payable on August 29, 2025, to shareholders of record on August 18, 2025 (Ex-Dividend Date: August 18, 2025).

The Fund’s Board of Trustees (the “Board”) has authorized a managed distribution plan (the “Plan”) pursuant to which the Fund makes monthly distributions to shareholders at the fixed rate of $0.0475 per share. The Plan is intended to provide shareholders with consistent distributions each month and is intended to narrow the discount between the market price and the net asset value (“NAV”) of the Fund’s common shares, but there can be no assurance that the Plan will be successful in doing so. The Fund is managed with a goal of generating as much of the distribution as possible from net ordinary income and short-term capital gains, that is consistent with the Fund’s investment strategy and risk profile. To the extent that sufficient distributable income is not available on a monthly basis, the Fund will distribute long-term capital gains and/or return of capital in order to maintain its managed distribution rate. A return of capital may occur, for example, when some or all of the money that was invested in the Fund is paid back to shareholders. A return of capital distribution does not necessarily reflect the Fund’s investment performance and should not be confused with “yield” or “income”. Even though the Fund may realize current year capital gains, such gains may be offset, in whole or in part, by the Fund’s capital loss carryovers from prior years.

The Board may amend the terms of the Plan or terminate the Plan at any time without prior notice to the Fund’s shareholders. The amendment or termination of the Plan could have an adverse effect on the market price of the Fund’s common shares. The Plan will be subject to the periodic review by the Board, including a yearly review of the fixed rate to determine if an adjustment should be made.

Shareholders should not draw any conclusions about the Fund’s investment performance from the amount of the current distribution or from the terms of the Plan. The amounts and sources of distributions reported herein are only estimates and are not being provided for tax reporting purposes. The actual amounts and sources of the amounts for tax reporting purposes will depend upon the Fund’s investment experience during the remainder of its fiscal year and may be subject to changes based on tax regulations. The Fund will send a Form 1099-DIV to shareholders for the calendar year that will describe how to report the Fund’s distributions for federal income tax purposes.

For further information on Templeton Emerging Markets Income Fund, please visit our web site at: www.franklintempleton.com

Franklin Resources, Inc. is a global investment management organization with subsidiaries operating as Franklin Templeton and serving clients in over 150 countries. Franklin Templeton’s mission is to help clients achieve better outcomes through investment management expertise, wealth management and technology solutions. Through its specialist investment managers, the company offers specialization on a global scale, bringing extensive capabilities in fixed income, equity, alternatives and multi-asset solutions. With more than 1,500 investment professionals, and offices in major financial markets around the world, the California-based company has over 75 years of investment experience and $1.61 trillion in assets under management as of June 30, 2025. For more information, please visit franklintempleton.com.

data.title: Arria NLG and Resolute Announce Strategic Partnership to Advance AI Across Government and Commercial Sectors
data.description:
Arria NLG and Resolute Announce Strategic Partnership to Advance AI Across Government and Commercial Sectors

Arria NLG, a global leader in Natural Language Generative AI and Agentic AI, is proud to announce a strategic partnership with Resolute, a distinguished advisory firm specializing in dual-use technologies, defense innovation, and commercial strategy.

For over a decade, Arria has architected, developed and deployed an advanced AI system to many Fortune 500 companies and governmental organizations including the US Department of Defense. Arria holds 45 patents in its IP portfolio providing unique and powerful AI technology.

Resolute and Arria have jointly identified a gap that Arria addresses in the merging of AI technologies. This is predominately for use cases where accuracy, control, security and compliance are of the highest standards. Some examples of this need are organizations who seek efficiency in automation while adhering to AI-governance standards. This partnership addresses growing market demand for both the government and commercial sectors.

“Partnering with Resolute accelerates our mission to bring intelligent, domain-aware analytics to critical sectors,” said Sharon Daniels, CEO of Arria NLG. “Their leadership in national security and commercial markets, combined with Arria’s proven AI orchestration and narrative intelligence, positions us to drive innovation where precision, scale and interpretability matter most.”

As part of the partnership, Mark “Turk” Powell—a seasoned expert in defense, commercial innovation, and capital strategy—will serve as Strategic Executive Advisor to Arria, providing strategic oversight and deep domain expertise across federal and enterprise programs. Powell brings extensive leadership in dual-use innovation and has advised on numerous large-scale commercial growth initiatives.

Resolute’s leadership includes Dionel Aviles, former Under Secretary of the Navy and senior defense industry executive, and Pete Peterson, a veteran in defense-focused innovation and investment. Their combined insight and national security experience will support the adoption of Agentic AI across mission-critical use cases.

“Arria is setting the standard by delivering secure technology that is both efficient and cost effective,” said Powell.

Together, Arria and Resolute shape the next generation of enterprise and government outcomes where real-time, high-stakes decisions demand both speed and clarity and trust. This partnership underscores a shared commitment to operationalizing AI for secure, scalable and mission-aligned outcomes—turning data into strategic advantage through intelligent automation.

About Arria
Arria serves Fortune 500 clients and government agencies worldwide with “knowledge-based” Agents used to autonomously deliver data-driven reporting, monitoring and alerts.

The Arria platform is purpose-built to orchestrate narrative generation with accuracy and full control—addressing the automation requirements for financial, compliance, audit, regulatory and critical business decision-making where rules and AI-governance are paramount. Arria delivers no-code, configurable Agents and specialized custom Agents each of which ensures the integrity of client narratives.

Arria can be deployed in the cloud or on premises, either dynamically within a dashboard or via process automation.

www.arria.com

About Resolute
Resolute is an elite advisory group helping organizations navigate the intersection of national security, emerging technology and commercial strategy. With decades of experience in defense, intelligence and capital markets, Resolute delivers tailored insight and results-driven execution.

www.resoluteteam.us

Media:
investor.relations@arria.com

data.title: Faster and Safer: XXKK Exchange Launches Fully Upgraded Web Platform, Delivering Smart Crypto Trading Experience in 2025
data.description:
DENVER, Aug. 07, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Leading cryptocurrency trading platform XXKK Exchange officially announces the launch of its fully upgraded web trading platform. This upgrade centers on enhancing user experience across key areas including account registration, KYC verification, crypto trading operations, digital asset management, security, and customer support. The new platform significantly improves the efficiency and safety of trading major cryptocurrencies such as Bitcoin, USDT, and Ethereum, empowering users to manage their digital assets smarter and more conveniently.

XXKK Exchange’s Global Markets Head, Emmalyn, stated:

"Our upgraded web platform is designed to address the evolving needs of crypto investors worldwide. By focusing on seamless registration, enhanced security, and efficient trading tools, we aim to deliver an exceptional experience that supports both beginners and professional traders in managing Bitcoin, USDT, and other digital assets with confidence."

Streamlined Registration and KYC Process to Lower Barriers for New Crypto Investors
Cryptocurrency investors often face complex registration and slow KYC verification on many trading platforms. How does XXKK’s upgraded web platform solve these common pain points? Compared to other crypto exchanges, what improvements make the registration and KYC process faster and easier?

XXKK has made comprehensive enhancements including:

  • Fast and Simple Account Registration: A simplified registration interface allows users to create a crypto trading account in minutes and start trading Bitcoin, Ethereum, USDT, and other popular digital assets immediately.
  • Automated KYC Verification: The platform uses an intelligent automated KYC system to reduce manual checks, significantly shortening approval times while ensuring compliance.
  • Localized Compliance for Global Markets: Tailored document requirements and integration with local compliance systems, especially optimized for the Indian market, help improve verification success rates.

These optimizations allow new users to quickly activate their accounts and deposit cryptocurrencies, achieving a seamless “ready-to-trade” experience that lowers the entry barrier to digital asset investing.

Multi-layered Security Architecture Ensures a Safe Crypto Trading Environment

Security is critical in cryptocurrency trading. XXKK’s upgraded web platform strengthens security protocols to safeguard users’ blockchain assets including Bitcoin, USDT, and other cryptocurrencies:

  • Two-Factor Authentication (2FA): Requires secondary verification for logins, withdrawals, and sensitive operations to prevent unauthorized access.
  • Biometric Authentication: Supports fingerprint and facial recognition for secure yet convenient login.
  • Real-time Risk Monitoring and Intelligent Controls: 24/7 account monitoring detects suspicious activities and automatically triggers protective measures to secure crypto assets.

Additionally, XXKK Exchange is committed to global regulatory compliance, holding multiple licenses including US MSB (License No. 31000222694535), Canada MSB (License No. M22420435), and St. Vincent FSA (License No. 3393). These licenses demonstrate its commitment to lawful operations and protecting users’ funds.

This comprehensive security framework protects users throughout the entire trading journey, ensuring a reliable experience for both novice and professional crypto traders.

Upgraded Web Platform Enables Efficient Bitcoin, USDT and Crypto Trading Anytime, Anywhere

XXKK’s new web platform is not just a trading portal but also an integrated tool featuring real-time cryptocurrency market data, technical analysis, and asset management. Users can track live Bitcoin, Ethereum, and USDT prices and analyze candlestick charts to make informed trading decisions quickly. The enhanced one-click buy/sell functions shorten transaction times, boosting crypto trading efficiency.
Built-in crypto wallets support seamless management of Bitcoin, USDT, and a wide range of digital assets, allowing users to deposit, withdraw, and transfer cryptocurrencies all within the platform. Price alerts and push notifications keep users informed of market movements, enabling them to seize trading opportunities instantly—truly a “crypto trading platform at your fingertips.”

Localized Customer Support Improves the Cryptocurrency Investment Experience

XXKK Exchange strengthens localized customer service, especially for Indian crypto investors. Hindi-speaking support assists with wallet setup, deposits, withdrawals, and other common issues, removing language barriers and improving service efficiency. Users can contact dedicated support directly through the platform or via WhatsApp for instant help.
Operating within local time zones, the support team ensures quick responses during peak Bitcoin trading and USDT transfer hours. From registration to KYC and daily trading, XXKK delivers end-to-end support for a smooth and secure crypto investment experience.

Join XXKK Now

Register for free and complete onboarding tasks to receive up to $8,888 in new user bonuses and start your journey to crypto wealth. Enjoy an additional 2% cashback on every USDT deposit.
Join XXKK Exchange today—capture market opportunities and experience low-cost, high-yield crypto trading.

Official website: www.xxkk.com
Customer support email: marketing@xxkk.com

data.title: Burger King-Owner Restaurant Brands International 2Q Profit Squeezed by Higher Commodity, Restaurant Costs — Update
data.description:
By Adriano Marchese 




  Burger King-owner Restaurant Brands International reported a lower second-quarter profit as the company faced higher commodity costs and restaurant expenses. 



  The decline in profit, which undershot Wall Street expectations, came as the company posted higher revenue overall, with growth at Burger King and Tim Hortons offsetting declines at Popeyes and Firehouse Subs. 



  Shares slipped 4.4% to $65.57. 



  Cost pressure was particularly strong in the U.S. where beef prices rose in the high teens in the first half of the year. Since beef makes up about a quarter of Burger King's domestic purchases, Chief Financial Officer Sami Siddiqui said the increase is driving mid-single-digit inflation to its costs of goods sold. 



  "Beef is about 25% of our cost basket and we're seeing around 15% inflation on the year to date," Siddiqui said on an earnings call. 



  For the quarter, net income dropped to $189 million, or 57 cents a share, compared with $280 million, or 88 cents a share, a year earlier. 



  Total operating expenses surged 36% to $1.93 billion. Income from operations, a key company metric which measures the cost of operating the business' everyday operations, fell to $483 million from $663 million. 



  Adjusted earnings were 94 cents, shy of analysts forecasts of 97 cents, according to FactSet. 



  Revenue rose 16% to $2.41 billion, ahead of analyst estimates, with system-wide comparable sales of 2.4%. Burger King comparable sales rose 1.3%, while Tim Hortons gained 3.4%. Popeyes and Firehouse Subs posted declines of 1.4% and 0.8%, respectively. 



  The company's international segment helped offset softer domestic performance. Burger King International stood out in the quarter with comparable sales that rose 4.1%, while Tim Hortons Canada rose 3.6%. 



  "The Burger King International business has been on a great growth trajectory," said Chief Executive Josh Kobza on the investor call, pointing to strong operations and franchisee relationships in Spain, Germany and the U.K. 



  China was another bright spot, where Kobza said that the return to positive same-store sales occurred faster than expected. The company is still working to find a buyer for the Burger King China business, which it acquired in February. 



  "The brand is just in a good place in a lot of these international markets," Kobza said. 



  Restaurant Brands reaffirmed its longer-term expectations, targeting a 3% comparable sales growth and 8% organic adjusted operating income growth through 2028. 



  Restaurant Brands also approved a share buyback plan of $1 billion from Sept. 15, 2025 to Sept. 30, 2027. This replaces its prior two-year repurchase authorization. 





  Write to Adriano Marchese at adriano.marchese@wsj.com 





  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  August 07, 2025 11:42 ET (15:42 GMT)


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: Investors can't afford to ignore the stagflation threat shadowing the market
data.description:
By William Watts

'The stagflation theme in markets is intensifying': Apollo's Slok

Stagflation fears haven't gone away.

The stock market bounced back from a wobble sparked by a closely watched economic gauge earlier this week, but investors should take care not to get caught napping by the potential threat of stagflation it helped underline, a closely followed Wall Street economist warned Thursday.

"The bottom line is that the stagflation theme in markets is intensifying," Torsten Slok, chief economist at Apollo Global Management, said in a note. Stagflation is a term referring to the unwelcome combination of stagnant economic growth and rising inflation.

Talk of stagflation heated up following the release Tuesday of the Institute for Supply Management's July gauge of services-sector activity. The prices-paid index, a measure of inflation, rose 2.4 points to 69.9 and hit a nearly three-year high, pointing to the potential for upward pressure on the consumer-price index (see chart below).

The ISM nonmanufacturing index itself fell to 50.1% in July from 50.8% a month earlier, signaling the economy's dominant services sector barely grew (a reading of less than 50% indicates a contraction in activity).

At the same time, employment growth is slowing and the unemployment rate is rising, Slok said. The "stagflation impulse" is the result of tariffs, deportations and a weakening U.S. dollar, he argued.

Lauren Goodwin, economist and chief market strategist at New York Life Investments, highlighted those factors and others in a note earlier this week.

"Trade policy is impacting prices; immigration policy is impacting labor supply; deregulation is providing yet another jolt to growth sectors; tax adjustments make capital investment easier to stomach. The combination of these factors with recent economic data signals a clear 'stagflation-lite'backdrop," she wrote.

Goodwin urged caution when it comes to longer-dated debt, arguing the yield on the 10-year Treasury note BX:TMUBMUSD10Y was likely to drift back toward 4.5%. The yield was at 4.22% on Thursday; yields and debt prices move opposite each other.

Stagflation would mean life remains complicated for Federal Reserve policymakers, Slok said. Should policymakers "focus on rising inflation and hike rates, or on slowing growth and cut rates?" he wrote.

Uncertainty encircles the ultimate impact the Trump administration's tariff measures will have on inflation in the services sector.

"The question is whether higher goods costs due to the trade war will translate into less demand for services and, therefore, downward pressure on inflation in the sector," Ian Lyngen and Vail Hartman, rates strategists at BMO Capital Markets, wrote in a Thursday note.

"That's not a difficult dynamic to envision; although we suspect that if those forces ultimately come into play, there will be lags in the timing," they wrote. In other words, next week's consumer-price index reading is unlikely to be the last word.

The central bank has left rates on hold in 2025 as it awaits clarity on the impact of tariffs on the inflation picture, enraging President Donald Trump, who has incessantly criticized Fed Chair Jerome Powell.

Last Friday's disappointing July jobs report and accompanying downward revisions to May and June data led investors to increase their expectations for a resumption of rate cuts. Fed-funds futures traders have priced in a 93% probability of a quarter-point cut at the next policy meeting in September, according to the CME FedWatch Tool, up from less than 40% a week ago. Traders see a slightly better than 50% probability of a total of three, quarter-point cuts by year-end.

Those rate-cut expectations, along with well-received corporate earnings and another bout of optimism over artificial-intelligence plays, have helped lift stocks, analysts said, with the S&P 500 SPX trading less than 1% away from its record close of 6,389.77 set on July 28; the large-cap benchmark is up 1.7% so far this week. The Nasdaq Composite COMP was up 3% for the week to date, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average DJIA lagged behind with a gain of 0.8%.

"The market is clearly expecting cuts, but the upside risks to inflation are significant, and investors should be carefully watching survey-based and market-based measures of inflation expectations," Slok wrote.

-William Watts

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  08-07-25 1145ET


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.
